Use of 푉= ொ ସ஠ఌ బ ௥ (accept use of 푉= ௞ொ ௥ ) and 푉= ௐ ொ Subtract W at 9.0 cm from W at 4.0 cm Or Subtract V at 9.0 cm from V at 4.0 cm Work done = 3.9 × 10 −6 J Example of calculation 푊 = − 4.5 × 10 ିଽ C×7.0 × 10 ିଽ C 4π× 8.85 × 10 ିଵଶ F m ିଵ × 0.040 m − 4.5 nC×7.0 nC 4π× 8.85 × 10 ିଵଶ F m ିଵ × 0.09 m = − 3.15 × 10 −6 J − − 7.08 × 10 −6 J Work done = 3.93 × 10 −6 J (1) (1) (1) Total for question 12 7 . [Accept 760 (m) if v = 52 m s −1 used] Example of calculation W = 1200 kg × 9.81 N kg −1 = 11772 N L = W / cos 휃 = 11772 N / cos 20° = 12527 N L h = L sin 휃 = 12527 N × sin 20° = 4285 N 4285 N = mv /r = 1200 kg (54 m s −1 ) / r r = 816 m [ r = 757 m if v = 52 m s −1 used] (1) (1) (1) (1) (1) Or Use of v = r and  = 2π/T t = 24 s (ecf from a(i)) [Accept correctly calculated values using value of r calculated in (i) with either v = 52 m s −1 or v = 54 m s −1 ] Example of calculation t = (2π × 816 m / 4)/ 54m s −1 t = 23.8 s (1) (1) Resultant upwards force Or lift is greater than weight Or vertical component of lift is now greater than weight Aeroplane will accelerate upwards (1) (1) Total for question 13 9 Number Answer Mark If no (external) unbalanced / resultant force acts Or in a closed system (1) (1) K = ½ mv Correct value for one object Not elastic collision because total E k before ≠ E k after Or elastic collision total E k before is (about) the same as E k after (all values must have been correctly calculated) Example of calculation Before E K = ½ mv = ½ × 0.85 kg × (1.30 m s −1 ) = 0.72 J After E K = ½ mv = ½ × 0.85 kg × (0.98 m s −1 ) = 0.41 J E K = ½ mv = ½ × 1.70 kg × (0.54 m s −1 ) = 0.25 J Total = 0.66 J (1) (1) (1) Use of trigonometry to find a component of momentum after collision Shows momentum before in x direction = momentum after in original direction Shows perpendicular component
